data.title: review-gate-bypass-sentinel.sh: python3 open().write() to test-gate-status -> sentinel pattern check does not block the write
data.description:
## Incident Overview
The bypass-sentinel (hooks/lib/review-gate-bypass-sentinel.sh) blocks direct writes to test-gate-status using shell command pattern matching: redirects (>), cp, mv, tee, echo/printf. However, a Python write using open().write() is not caught by any of these patterns.

## Actual Behavior
Running: python3 -c "open('/tmp/workflow-plugin-.../test-gate-status', 'w').write('passed\n')"
The command contains 'test-gate-status' so the sentinel's outer check triggers, but none of the write-pattern regexes match (no shell redirect operator, no cp/mv/tee/echo/printf). The write succeeds silently, bypassing the integrity gate.

## Expected Behavior
Any write to test-gate-status — regardless of the tool used — should be blocked unless the command contains record-test-status.sh.

## Fix Options
Option A: Add a python3 write pattern to the sentinel: [[ "$COMMAND" =~ python3.*open.*test-gate-status ]] or similar.
Option B: Add a general catch-all: when the command contains 'test-gate-status' and does NOT contain 'record-test-status.sh', block any command that could write to a file (python3, ruby, perl, node, awk with output, etc.).
Option B is more robust but requires enumerating interpreters. Option A is targeted and low-risk.
